name: Subiaco
description_short: Subiaco Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving five Arkansas counties since 1948, offering auto loans, agricultural loans, savings accounts, and online banking.
description_long:
Subiaco Federal Credit Union was founded on October 21, 1948, by seven charter members and officially approved on December 8, 1948. The credit union began serving Subiaco and a 5-mile radius with a mission to bring the credit union concept to the community. Over 75 years later, it remains a locally rooted financial institution operating two physical locations in Arkansas.

Subiaco FCU offers a range of loan products including auto loans (as low as 6.00% APR), agricultural loans (as low as 6.49% APR), and unsecured loans (as low as 13.00% APR). On the deposit side, they provide savings accounts, certificates of deposit (6-month at 0.25% APY, 12-month at 1.00% APY), and IRA accounts. The credit union also features online banking with home banking, alerts, e-statements, a skip-a-pay option for loan payment flexibility, and mobile apps for iOS and Android.

What distinguishes Subiaco FCU is its hyperlocal membership model—eligibility is restricted to individuals living or working in Logan, Franklin, Johnson, Scott, and Yell Counties in Arkansas, or those with immediate family already holding membership. The credit union emphasizes personalized, hometown service and offers ancillary services like notary public, money orders, fax/copy service, direct deposits, and payroll deductions. Membership requires only a $1 joining fee and $25 minimum balance, with lifetime membership status regardless of relocation.

Subiaco FCU is best suited for Arkansas residents in its five-county service area seeking competitive loan rates and personalized service without large national institution overhead. However, prospective members should note the strict geographic membership restrictions, limited branch footprint (two locations), and modest deposit rates that reflect typical credit union offerings. The featured rates shown are effective as of April 1, 2025, and are subject to change.
services: Online banking and home banking platform
pros: Low auto loan rates starting at 6.00% APR
Specialized agricultural loan product at 6.49% APR for rural borrowers
Membership requires only $1 fee and $25 minimum balance with lifetime eligibility
Free online banking with home banking, alerts, and e-statements
Skip-a-pay loan flexibility option available to borrowers
Extensive service hours including Saturday drive-thru (9 AM-12 PM)
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure prioritizes member interests over profit
cons: Membership restricted to five specific Arkansas counties, excluding most consumers
Only two physical branch locations (Subiaco and Paris), limiting accessibility
Deposit rates are very low (0.25%-1.00% APY on CDs and IRAs)
Unsecured loan rates start at 13.00% APR, which is moderately high
Limited online visibility and no information about credit monitoring or additional digital products
best_for: Farmers and agricultural workers in Logan, Franklin, Johnson, Scott, and Yell Counties seeking specialized farm loans
Arkansas residents in the five-county service area looking for personalized, locally-based credit union membership
Borrowers seeking low auto loan rates with small-town customer service and flexible payment options
diagnosis: Subiaco FCU is ideal for Arkansas residents within its five-county service area who prioritize personalized, community-focused banking and competitive loan rates. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership eligibility; consumers outside Logan, Franklin, Johnson, Scott, and Yell Counties (or without family members as members) cannot join, making it inaccessible to most people.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Subiaco — Credit Union in Subiaco, AR | CreditDoc
meta_description: Subiaco — NCUA-insured credit union in Subiaco, AR. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
